title: Tetrapterys phlomoides
taxonomy: Tetrapterys phlomoides belongs to the family Malpighiaceae, commonly known as the Barbados gooseberry family. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Tetrapterys. The genus Tetrapterys is part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Tetrapterys phlomoides is native to South America, specifically in countries such as Brazil and Colombia. The classification of Tetrapterys phlomoides reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Malpighiaceae family.

intro: Tetrapterys phlomoides, also known as the "Four-winged Tetrapterys", is a species of plant in the family Malpighiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The leaves and stems of Tetrapterys phlomoides have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetrapterys phlomoides is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used to make herbal capsules and tinctures.
